task_completion.description: Verify that the correct blog post title has been entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'The Future of Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare' in the main title input field of the blog post editor.

task_completion.description: Verify that the blog post content is written, appears to be at least 100 words/characters, and includes the required keywords.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main content area of the editor. There should be a substantial paragraph or multiple paragraphs discussing AI in healthcare. Scan the text to visually confirm the presence of the keywords: 'artificial intelligence', 'healthcare', 'diagnostics', 'treatment', and 'patient care'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Technology' category has been assigned to the post.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Categories panel (usually on the right sidebar). The checkbox next to 'Technology' should be checked.

task_completion.description: Verify that the four required tags have been added to the post.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Tags panel (usually on the right sidebar). The tags 'AI', 'healthcare', 'technology', and 'featured' should be visible as added tags.

task_completion.description: Verify that the blog post has been published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Post published' success notification, a 'View Post' link, or observe that the main action button says 'Update' instead of 'Publish', indicating the post is no longer a draft and is live.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the web browser GUI to create the post.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with a web browser (e.g., Chrome, Firefox) navigating the CMS interface, rather than terminal windows executing scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent is using the actual blog platform editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should be a recognizable CMS post editor (like WordPress), not a local text editor (like Notepad, Gedit, or VS Code).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use command-line shortcuts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not display commands like 'wp post create', curl requests to an API, or SQL INSERT statements used to create the post programmatically.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Title: 'The Future of Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare'. Expected Category: 'Technology'. Expected Tags: 'AI', 'healthcare', 'technology', 'featured'. Expected Status: 'publish'. Content must be at least 100 characters/words and include the keywords: 'artificial intelligence', 'healthcare', 'diagnostics', 'treatment', 'patient care'.
